title: Fire Code Complaint Records – Jiminy Peak Unit 844 (Jan 31, 2026)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law (M.G.L. c. 66, § 10), I hereby request copies of all records related to a fire and life safety complaint submitted on or about January 31, 2026, concerning obstructed means of egress in the interior hallway outside Unit 844 at Jiminy Peak Mountain Resort, located in Hancock, Massachusetts.

This request includes, but is not limited to:

• Any complaint logs, incident reports, or intake records reflecting receipt of the complaint  
• Any internal or external communications (including emails, notes, or memoranda) referencing the complaint  
• Any records reflecting actions taken, inspections conducted, or determinations made  
• Any correspondence between the Town of Hancock (including the Fire Department and/or any municipal office) and Jiminy Peak Mountain Resort regarding this issue  
• Any records indicating whether the complaint was reviewed, investigated, or closed  

The timeframe for this request is January 31, 2026 through the present.

If any responsive records are withheld, please provide the specific statutory basis for each denial.

I request that all responsive records be provided in electronic format where available.

I also request a waiver of any fees, as this request concerns fire and life safety conditions affecting building occupants and is made in the public interest.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your response within 10 business days.

Sincerely,  
Michael T. Pace

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Thomas Pace
